steps.content: The car must have the sticker for approval in the Mechanical / EV Inspection before performing the Rain Test.
The car must be complete (ready to Drive).
All accompanying members must be using PPE.
The vehicle must be off the ground in its jacking points.
No driver or member is inside the vehicle.
The vehicle must be completely off.
Instruct the ESO to activate the tractive system (TS).
Verify the RML is flashing after TS activation.
Inspect TSSI/IMD status and verify there are no insulation faults before induced-fault testing.
Instruct the ESO to connect a resistor to the TSMP to induce an insulation fault.
Inspect TSSI/IMD status and verify the induced insulation fault is detected.
Verify the IMD fault-detection path remains operational after fault injection.
Reset the system after induced-fault verification.
Instruct the ESO to activate the TS again after reset.
Before spray starts, verify no personnel are near the vehicle.
Start the water spray with the vehicle on jack stands.
Maintain continuous water spray for 120 seconds.
Stop the water spray.
Monitor the vehicle for 120 seconds after spray stops.
Verify the vehicle does not enter a fault condition for the full 240-second test window.
If Step 20 passes, mark the Rain Test as successful and complete.
Contact the EV inspection leader to obtain the approval sticker.
